_comment: GREEKTH.TTF legacy ASCII -> Unicode polytonic Greek mapping. Evidence tiers: 'verified' = confirmed by real vocabulary/teaching text in 1_ALPHAB.TBK rendering to known Greek words; 'rendered' = read from glyph-sheet rendering of the font, not yet confirmed by word evidence; 'unknown' = glyph exists but unidentified. Diacritic codes FOLLOW the vowel they modify. Verify 'rendered' and 'unknown' tiers against DOSBox before bulk conversion. UPDATED 2026-07-20 (chapter 2 extraction): '#' and '[' promoted to verified; Greek punctuation ';' ':' identified; 'v' witnessed as a provisional base letter; '!' excluded as Hebrew-region contamination.
lowercase_evidence: verified (glyph render + vocab words: qeo<j=θεός, kardi<a=καρδία, le<gw=λέγω, profh<thj=προφήτης, a@nqrwpoj=ἄνθρωπος; j=final sigma verified by every -oj/-hj ending)
uppercase_evidence: rendered (glyph sheet; Xristo<j=Χριστός verifies X). J unconfirmed -- possibly capital final-sigma slot or unused.

%.name: alpha + iota subscript (composite)
%.evidence: skoti<%=σκοτίᾳ; iota-subscript teaching page pairs % with 'a as in father'

^.name: eta + iota subscript (composite)
^.evidence: a]rx^?=ἀρχῇ; teaching page pairs ^ with 'e as in obey'

&.name: omega + iota subscript (composite)
&.evidence: au]t&?=αὐτῷ; teaching page pairs & with 'o as in home'

>.name: grave (varia)
>.evidence: Greek Keyboard dialog: shift+period keycap shows grave; spelling-exercise tile row includes grave

*.name: diaeresis (dialytika)
*.evidence: Greek Keyboard dialog: shift+8 keycap shows diaeresis

#.name: smooth breathing + circumflex
#.evidence: h#lqen=ἦλθεν (Rule 5 chart, glossed 'he went', Jn 1:7); h#n=ἦν in kai> qeo>j h#n annotated '(two graves and a circumflex)' (Jn 1:1). a#postoloj appears ONLY as a deliberately mis-accented Accent Rule Drill item.

[.name: rough breathing (dasia) -- second slot
[.evidence: PROMOTED from provisional: chapter 2 teaching text prints 'Rough breathing ( [ )'; ui[o<j=υἱός, u[pe<r=ὑπέρ, r[h?ma=ῥῆμα, o[ lo<goj=ὁ λόγος. Coexists with '"' (chapter 1 evidence ui"o<j) -- the book uses BOTH slots for the same mark.

unknown_codes._note: Remaining unidentified codes after chapter 2. '$' did not appear in any chapter-2 Greek word (likely a breathing+accent combo awaiting a later-chapter witness, e.g. rough+circumflex). '{ } | ~' witnessed only inside font-metric/table binary junk, '\\' only in DOS file paths, '=' only in OpenScript assignments -- all three may not be font codes at all. Do not convert strings containing these codes silently.

conversion_rules: Diacritic codes follow the vowel: process left-to-right, attach combining marks to preceding base character, then normalize to NFC (precomposed polytonic).
Composite codes % ^ & already include the base vowel -- do not also emit the vowel.
j is final sigma; s is medial sigma. Trust the source encoding, do not auto-finalize.
Any string containing an unknown_codes character must be routed to a manual-review list, never silently converted.
']' doubles as the coronis in crasis (ka]gw< = κἀγώ, same glyph as smooth breathing) and, when standalone between spaces, as the elision apostrophe (di ] au]tou? = δι᾽ αὐτοῦ).
Initial breathing codes may PRECEDE a capital vowel (']En', ']Egw<', ']Axaia'): attach to the following base letter.
_keyboard_layout_note: Greek Keyboard dialog (verified by photo): physical QWERTY keys type legacy codes 1:1 (Q=theta, W=omega, Y=psi, C=xi, J=final sigma, etc.); shifted number row and punctuation carry the diacritics. Confirms the font-map covers keyboard entry as well as stored text.

;.name: Greek question mark (erotimatiko)
;.evidence: Punctuation Marks page: 'Question Mark ( ; )  lo<goj;'. NOTE: U+037E NFC-normalizes to ASCII ';' -- store the NFC form.

:.name: Greek colon / ano teleia (raised dot)
:.evidence: Punctuation Marks page: 'Colon ( : )  lo<goj:'. NOTE: U+0387 NFC-normalizes to U+00B7 MIDDLE DOT -- store the NFC form.

v.name: nu (alternate slot?)
v.evidence: SINGLE witness: tou]voma = τοὔνομα (crasis of to onoma; audio file B_TOUVON.WAV). Weak -- verify against DOSBox rendering before trusting in bulk conversion.

excluded_codes.!: NOT a Greek font code. Appears only inside embedded HEBREW-font data regions in 2_ACCENT.TBK (Hebrew tutor shared resources: 'Hbaz! to sacrifice', 'fsan! to depart', vocab with Hiphil/Niphal stem labels). The extraction pipeline must detect and exclude Hebrew regions (tell-tales: Hebrew glosses, stem labels (Hi)/(Ni), field names HebrewWord/HideHebrew).
